app.name: Gamma
app.bestFor: The fastest path to a polished first draft — a finished, web-style deck in under 90 seconds with strong visual consistency.
app.rankBasis: The most consistent #1–2 pick across the three 2026 AI-presentation roundups in this file, with strong community mindshare for rapid first drafts and web-native sharing.
app.pricingFree: Free plan — limited AI credits and basic presentation generation
app.pricingPaid: Plus and Pro tiers available; the current official page does not expose stable plan pricing in static content
pros: exceptionally fast polished first drafts
strong visual consistency without manual layout work
easy web sharing and lightweight collaboration
cons: less precise slide-level control than native PowerPoint
PowerPoint exports can require cleanup
credit and export limits make the free tier best for evaluation

app.name: Plus AI
app.bestFor: Professional users working inside Google Slides and PowerPoint — generate, rewrite and reformat slides natively.
app.rankBasis: A frequent top-three recommendation in 2026 comparisons for professional deck work, differentiated by native Google Slides and PowerPoint operation rather than a separate editor.
app.pricingPaid: Basic $10/user/mo billed annually ($15 monthly) — 1,500 AI credits/mo in Google Slides and PowerPoint
features: Native Google Slides and PowerPoint add-ins
pros: keeps output in familiar editable slide applications
strong rewriting and reformatting of existing decks
reviewers value professional layouts and workflow fit
cons: only a 7-day trial rather than a standing free plan
credit usage varies by task and can be hard to predict
advanced document upload and branding require higher tiers

app.name: Canva
app.bestFor: Design polish with minimal effort — AI-assisted layouts and templates for visually clean, professional slides.
app.rankBasis: The broadest-adoption design platform in the set, with more than 200 million monthly users and thousands of independent reviews; every cited 2026 roundup includes it for templates and visual polish.
app.pricingFree: Free — core presentation editor, templates and limited AI usage
app.pricingPaid: Canva Pro and Business pricing is localized by country; paid tiers add premium assets, brand tools and higher AI limits
features: Magic Design and AI media tools
pros: huge template and stock-asset ecosystem
accessible to non-designers
strong collaboration and multi-format content reuse
cons: AI-generated decks can look generic without manual editing
large template library makes consistency harder to govern
PowerPoint export may shift fonts, animations or layouts

app.name: Microsoft Copilot for PowerPoint
app.bestFor: Teams living in PowerPoint who want AI generation, redesign suggestions and Agent Mode inside the world's most-used deck tool.
app.rankBasis: Benefits from PowerPoint's dominant enterprise installed base and appears in 2026 roundups for native workflow integration, though independent reviews remain mixed on output quality versus specialist generators.
app.pricingPaid: Microsoft 365 Copilot is a paid add-on to an eligible Microsoft 365 plan; public price varies by business or enterprise agreement
pros: works directly in the standard enterprise presentation tool
can ground drafts in existing Word files and company content
retains native PowerPoint editability and governance
cons: requires both an eligible Microsoft 365 plan and Copilot license
generated decks often need substantial visual and factual review
feature availability varies by tenant, channel and administrator

app.name: SlidesAI
app.bestFor: Turning raw text or a script into a full deck — outlines, visuals and speaker notes — inside Google Slides.
app.rankBasis: Included in multiple 2026 presentation roundups and reports a large user base, but has a smaller independent review corpus than Canva or Microsoft.
app.pricingFree: Free — 1 AI-generated presentation/mo with limited input and AI credits
app.pricingPaid: Pro $12/mo on annual billing — more presentations, larger inputs, document uploads and higher AI limits
pros: quickly turns long text into an editable deck
runs inside existing slide editors
simple onboarding with a real no-card free plan
cons: free plan allows very little monthly generation
design quality and text density still need manual cleanup
advanced exports and higher limits require paid plans

app.name: Prezent.ai
app.bestFor: Enterprise teams producing on-brand investor decks and business reviews without leaning on designers.
app.rankBasis: A specialized enterprise entrant cited by one source here; it has strong life-sciences customer claims but much thinner independent review and broad-community evidence than the top five.
app.pricingPaid: Enterprise custom pricing — contact sales; free trial available
pros: strong brand-control and enterprise template workflows
deep specialization in life sciences and business communication
large reusable slide library and administrative controls
cons: no public self-serve price
sales-led deployment is excessive for individuals and small teams
limited independent review volume outside its target industries

skill.name: reveal.js
skill.bestFor: The de-facto standard open-source HTML presentation framework (~72k stars) — Markdown authoring, PDF export, speaker notes, LaTeX and auto-animate. The most-adopted tool in the entire category.
skill.rankBasis: The most-starred dedicated presentation framework at about 72k GitHub stars, with long-running adoption, active maintenance and a large plugin/theme ecosystem.
features: Nested slides, notes and overview mode
pros: mature, highly customizable web presentation standard
large ecosystem and excellent documentation
works well for technical talks and interactive demos
cons: requires web-development skills for deep customization
browser rendering and print-to-PDF can vary
does not create native editable PowerPoint files

skill.name: Slidev
skill.bestFor: The modern developer favorite (~47.8k stars): Markdown-driven decks powered by Vite/Vue 3 with live coding, Mermaid, KaTeX and PPTX/PDF/PNG export. Also ships its own official Agent Skill (see below).
skill.rankBasis: The fastest-growing modern developer slide framework here at about 47.8k GitHub stars, with active 2026 development and strong conference-talk adoption.
features: Live code, Monaco and presenter tools
pros: excellent developer experience and rapid local preview
rich technical-content components out of the box
theme and addon ecosystem supports reusable decks
cons: Vue/Vite concepts add a learning curve
PPTX export is less faithful and editable than native PowerPoint authoring
complex decks can depend on browser-specific rendering

skill.name: impress.js
skill.bestFor: Prezi-style CSS3 3D and zoom transitions with full HTML/CSS/JS control (~38.2k stars). Very high adoption, though less actively maintained than reveal.js and Slidev.
skill.rankBasis: A historically influential presentation framework at about 38.2k GitHub stars, still maintained but less central in current developer workflows than reveal.js or Slidev.
pros: distinctive nonlinear visual storytelling
complete browser-level styling control
small core with no proprietary runtime
cons: motion-heavy decks can distract audiences or cause discomfort
authoring spatial layouts is labor-intensive
smaller modern ecosystem and fewer built-in content tools

skill.name: pptx (Anthropic official skill)
skill.bestFor: Anthropic's first-party SKILL.md for building, reading and editing real .pptx/.potx files — HTML-to-PPTX generation plus raw OOXML editing. The strongest skill in the category and the one that produces genuinely editable PowerPoint; lives in the 163.9k-star anthropics/skills repo.
skill.rankBasis: The official Anthropic PPTX skill inside the roughly 163.9k-star anthropics/skills repository; it is the reference agent workflow for editable PowerPoint, though parent stars do not measure the skill alone.
features: Create and edit native PPTX files
pros: produces genuinely editable PowerPoint rather than screenshots
official workflow includes collision and overflow checks
supports both high-level generation and low-level repair
cons: requires a compatible agent plus Node, Python and rendering dependencies
high-quality decks still need iterative visual inspection
repository popularity cannot be attributed to this skill alone

skill.name: remark
skill.bestFor: A simple in-browser, Markdown-driven slideshow (~13k stars) for people comfortable with HTML/CSS. A long-standing lightweight option.
skill.rankBasis: A long-standing lightweight Markdown slideshow project at about 13k GitHub stars, but its last repository push was in 2024 and current momentum is limited.
pros: minimal setup and a very small conceptual surface
plain Markdown makes decks easy to version
works from a single HTML page
cons: maintenance activity is low
fewer modern export, animation and component features
no native PowerPoint editing workflow

skill.name: Spectacle
skill.bestFor: A React/JSX-based presentation library (~10.1k stars) from Formidable Labs, strong for live code demos and the React ecosystem; actively maintained.
skill.rankBasis: The main React-native presentation framework at about 10.1k GitHub stars, with active maintenance and clear adoption among JavaScript conference speakers.
pros: natural fit for React teams
components enable interactive technical demonstrations
reusable themes and programmatic content
cons: requires a React build toolchain
more code-heavy than Markdown-first alternatives
not designed to produce editable PPTX files

skill.name: python-pptx
skill.bestFor: The foundational Python library for programmatically generating and reading .pptx (~3.5k stars). Star count is modest but real-world adoption is enormous — it is the engine the Anthropic pptx skill and most PPTX tooling depend on, so it ranks above higher-starred JS tools on adoption depth.
skill.rankBasis: Only about 3.5k GitHub stars, but it is the dominant Python PPTX library with deep downstream usage in document automation and agent tooling; repository development is comparatively slow.
pros: creates standard editable PowerPoint files
stable API and broad Python ecosystem usage
excellent foundation for repeatable deck automation
cons: no rendering engine, so visual QA needs PowerPoint or LibreOffice
many advanced PowerPoint features are unsupported or low-level
maintenance cadence is slower than newer presentation projects

skill.name: Marp (marp-cli)
skill.bestFor: A mature Markdown-to-slides ecosystem (~3.7k stars for the CLI, plus a popular VS Code extension) exporting HTML/PDF/PPTX. Lower star count than the leaders but a well-adopted Markdown-native toolchain.
skill.rankBasis: The CLI has about 3.7k GitHub stars, supplemented by a widely used VS Code extension and active 2026 releases; adoption is broader than the CLI star count alone suggests.
pros: simple Markdown workflow with strong editor integration
reliable multi-format command-line export
good fit for version-controlled documentation teams
cons: PPTX output is not as editable as a natively authored deck
custom visual design requires CSS
interactive browser content does not transfer to static exports

skill.name: Slidev official skill
skill.bestFor: A first-party Agent Skill (SKILL.md) shipped inside the Slidev repo that teaches an agent Slidev syntax, animations, diagrams, themes and export. Official skill from a 47.8k-star project; adoption of the skill itself is still new, hence medium.
skill.rankBasis: An official skill in the actively maintained 47.8k-star Slidev repository; it has strong project provenance but no separate adoption or independent review count yet.
pros: maintained alongside the framework it teaches
gives agents concrete Slidev-specific conventions
covers technical content and export behavior
cons: new skill with no independent usage signal
requires Slidev and its Node/Vue toolchain
inherits Slidev's limited native PPTX editability

skill.name: academic-pptx-skill (Gabberflast)
skill.bestFor: A community SKILL.md (~716 stars) that layers academic-deck discipline (action titles, situation-complication-resolution, citation standards) on top of Anthropic's pptx skill. Well-scoped complement rather than a standalone generator.
skill.rankBasis: A focused community skill at about 717 GitHub stars with active 2026 updates; its niche academic scope is clear, but independent review evidence remains limited.
features: Conference, seminar and defense deck patterns
pros: addresses academic storytelling rather than only file generation
clear citation and argument-structure rules
complements the official Anthropic PPTX workflow
cons: not a standalone renderer or PPTX engine
small community and limited external validation
opinionated communication rules may need adapting by discipline

notes: Skill/Repo track rebuilt and re-ranked by real quality/reputation/adoption (verified GitHub stars, official status) rather than by form factor — 'kind' is a tag only. It now mixes notable open-source repos and genuine SKILL.md Agent Skills. reveal.js leads (~72k stars) as the unambiguous open-source presentation leader, with Slidev and impress.js close behind. Anthropic's official pptx skill ranks 4th and is the top skill: unlike the JS frameworks it produces genuinely editable .pptx files, built on python-pptx (7th). Slidev also ships its own official skill. All stars verified via the GitHub API. No MCP servers; the prior MCP entries (Office-PowerPoint-MCP-Server, SlideSpeak/SlidesGPT MCP) were removed as out of scope.
